text.title: Structural Design Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Adapter introduced for code that doesn't cross interface boundaries.
antipattern.fix: Reject; reintroduce when a real interface mismatch surfaces.

antipattern.symptom: Function decorated by 5 decorators; debugging requires unwrapping each layer.
antipattern.fix: Flatten into a middleware pipeline (FastAPI dependencies, Express middleware, ASP.NET filters); cap decorators at 2.

antipattern.symptom: `OrdersFacade` exposes 47 unrelated methods spanning checkout, refunds, inventory, payments.
antipattern.fix: Split into 4-5 cohesive facades or refactor to domain services.

antipattern.symptom: Flyweight introduced for 500 instances; complexity 5×, memory saving <1MB.
antipattern.fix: Revert Flyweight; require measured ≥10% RSS attribution before reintroduction.
